title: Quick check: deleting a node with one child

questions.text: Delete node 25 from the original tree (root 20, left subtree rooted at 10 with children 5 and 15, right subtree rooted at 30 with children 25, which has a right child 27, and 35). Node 25 has only one child, 27, and no left child. After deletion, which node becomes 30's left child?
questions.explanation: With only one child, there is no ordering conflict to resolve: 25's single child, 27, simply takes 25's place. 30's left pointer is redirected straight to 27, and no value needs copying.
